plang.note: The v4 StreamCallback 502 did not recur — confirms it was external infra.

coverage.note: v5 changed only tests + 1 docstring (this.Unary.cs). Loader.cs sealed gate now has all 3 enforcement sites covered.

findings.issue: v4-F1: Loader sealed gate tested at 1 of 3 sites. coder v5 added LoadDll_SealedNameAsRendererTypeName (pass-2 renderer, SignatureRendererShadow.dll) and LoadDll_InferredSealedName (pass-1 inferred-name, CallbackInferredShadow.dll).
findings.impact: RESOLVED. Tester-reproduced mutation: neutralizing :122 + :101 (leaving :88 intact) failed exactly those 2 tests of 11; explicit-attr test stayed green (control). Both assert ErrorKey==TypeLoadCollision + ErrorMessage contains the sealed name, so no TypeLoadCoverage slip.

findings.issue: v4-F2: Sqrt_NegativeInput_Fails asserted only Success.IsFalse. coder v5 added Assert Error.Key == "ArithmeticError".
findings.impact: RESOLVED. Tester-reproduced mutation: DoSqrt throwing DivideByZeroException now fails the test at the Error.Key assertion (was green under the same mutation in v4).

findings.issue: v4-F3: no baseline-tests.md for v3/v4. coder v5 wrote one (HEAD a58dcfeee, all-green).
v4-F4: stale Sqrt docstring said validation lives 'inside the handler'. coder v5 corrected it to 'throws ArithmeticException -> Wrap -> ArithmeticError, one canonical key'.
coder/v5/report.md is an empty file; the detail lives only in the commit message.
findings.impact: Non-blocking process nit; no effect on test quality.

summary: coder v5 resolved all four tester-v4 findings. The two review-driven sealed-gate tests (renderer-pass + inferred-name) and the strengthened sqrt Error.Key assert were each independently mutation-confirmed by the tester to go red when their target gate/key breaks, with the explicit-attribute test held as an untouched green control. C# 3636/3636, plang 248/248 (the v4 502 flake did not recur). One non-blocking nit: empty coder/v5/report.md. No false greens remain — PASS.
